fields.label: Projects limit
fields.description: Number of items to retrieve in projects list query

fields.label: Username
fields.description: Username of the user that owns the Azure DevOps PAT. May be needed for some versions of Azure DevOps Server.

relationships.description: Azure DevOps PAT connection: the organization/collection URL (jsonData.url) and the personal access token (secureJsonData.patToken). By default azuredevops.NewPatConnection(url, patToken) sends the PAT as HTTP Basic auth (empty username + PAT) on every request (pkg/plugin/plugin.go:74).

Optional Basic auth username: when jsonData.username is non-empty the backend builds an explicit Basic auth header via azuredevops.CreateBasicAuthHeaderValue(username, patToken) and normalizes the URL (lowercased, trailing slash trimmed) instead of the default empty-username PAT connection — needed for some Azure DevOps Server versions (pkg/plugin/plugin.go:76-84).

instructions.msg: The Azure DevOps datasource has a single authentication method: a personal access token (PAT). jsonData.authType is fixed to 'patToken' (the only supported value); the backend declares the field but does not branch on it (marked 'Not in use yet', pkg/plugin/settings.go:11). Configure the organization/collection URL plus the PAT.

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"url":"https://dev.azure.com/<organization>","authType":"patToken"},"secureJsonData":{"patToken":"<your-azure-devops-pat>"}}. Both jsonData.url and secureJsonData.patToken are required: an empty url fails settings load with 'invalid URL' and an empty patToken fails with 'invalid PAT' (pkg/plugin/settings.go:19-27,35-45).

Optional jsonData.username: when set, the backend authenticates with an explicit HTTP Basic header CreateBasicAuthHeaderValue(username, patToken) and lowercases + trims the trailing slash of the URL; when empty it uses azuredevops.NewPatConnection, which is empty-username PAT Basic auth. Set username only if your Azure DevOps Server version requires it (pkg/plugin/plugin.go:76-84).

secureJsonData.patToken is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the token is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.patToken (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.patToken.

Connection/URL rules: jsonData.url is stored in jsonData (NOT the datasource root url) and must be the full Azure DevOps organization/collection URL — e.g. https://dev.azure.com/<organization> for Azure DevOps Services or https://<server>/<collection> for Azure DevOps Server. jsonData.projectsLimit (optional) caps how many items the projects-list query returns; any value < 1 is coerced to 100 (pkg/plugin/settings.go:46-48).
